target: Issue #239 naiaKey credential flow redesign (fat shell → lean shell). Current: shell passes naiaKey in every chat_request; Proposed: shell sends auth_update once, agent holds naiaKey internally. 5-file scope: agent/src/index.ts, agent/src/providers/factory.ts, shell/src/lib/chat-service.ts, shell/src/components/OnboardingWizard.tsx, shell/src/components/ChatPanel.tsx. 6 open questions: startup resilience, backward compat, disk persistence, TTS dependency, auth_update timing race, scope creep.

description: TTS silent breakage: effectiveNaiaKey at index.ts:851 becomes undefined when top-level reqNaiaKey removed and ProviderConfig.naiaKey also removed; TTS path has no access to module-level agent state
rationale: All 3 reviewers found TTS naiaKey dependency (platform-specialist CRITICAL at index.ts:851, reasoning-auditor HIGH at chat-service.ts:144+index.ts:1076, slop-detector MEDIUM at index.ts:851)

description: ProviderConfig.naiaKey dual channel: factory.ts:89 routes via config.naiaKey (inside provider object), not top-level naiaKey; 5-file scope removing only SendChatOptions.naiaKey leaves credential still in payload
rationale: slop-detector role was SLOP verification on named claims; this gap was not a named claim; 2/3 supporters

description: protocol.ts missing from 5-file scope: auth_update type must be added to AgentRequest union (protocol.ts:156-168) and parseRequest whitelist (protocol.ts:170-194)
rationale: 2/3 supporters; slop-detector scope was claim verification not gap finding

description: Startup resilience (Q1) unresolved: agent module-level state is ephemeral; naia_auth_complete is one-time; no re-delivery on agent restart; Q1+Q3 dependency not made explicit
naia_auth_complete scope too narrow: 13 files reference event (SettingsTab, App.tsx, channel-sync, browser.rs, lib.rs); payload change may break more than 5 listed files
rationale: 2/3 supporters; platform-specialist did not raise as formal finding

description: factory.ts line attribution minor imprecision (87-93 vs 89-93)
rationale: domain-consistent finding for slop-detector role; no strike

evidence: ChatPanel.tsx:680 sets naiaKey inside provider object; factory.ts:89 routes via config.naiaKey=providerConfig.naiaKey; top-level reqNaiaKey only used as TTS fallback at index.ts:851. Both paths independently confirmed in code.

description: ProviderConfig.naiaKey dual channel confirmed by Phase 3 arbitration
rationale: Phase 3 arbitration read ChatPanel.tsx:680, factory.ts:89, index.ts:380-417, types.ts:8 and confirmed both naiaKey paths are real and independent
